name: Andorra (Principality of Andorra / Principat d'Andorra)
summary: Andorra is a Western European civil-law constitutional co-principality (uniquely co-headed by the Bishop of Urgell in Spain and the President of France as Co-Princes) whose family-law framework operates under the Civil Code (Codi de la Família) and Qualified Law on Marriage 2017 (Llei Qualificada del Matrimoni). Parental authority (potestat parental) and child custody are governed by Civil Code arts. 27-58 and Law 14/2019 on Children's and Adolescents' Rights. The Superior Court of Justice (Tribunal Superior de Justícia) is the apex court for civil and criminal matters; the Constitutional Court (Tribunal Constitucional) operates separate constitutional review. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the Court of Magistrates (Tribunal de Batlles). Psychology profession is regulated through the Andorran Order of Psychologists. Andorra is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the superior-interest-of-the-child standard codified in Law 14/2019. Andorra acceded to the Hague Convention 1980 effective 1 July 2011. Andorra is a Council of Europe member subject to ECHR jurisdiction.

statutory_framework.citation: Civil Code (Codi de la Família) arts. 27-58
statutory_framework.title: Civil Code — Parental authority
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Civil Code Family Code drawing on Catalan civil-law substantive heritage. Arts. 27-58 govern potestat parental and child custody.

statutory_framework.citation: Law 14/2019 on Children's and Adolescents' Rights
statutory_framework.title: Law on Children's and Adolescents' Rights
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Law on children's rights aligned with UNCRC obligations.

apex_courts.name: Superior Court of Justice (Tribunal Superior de Justícia)
apex_courts.role: Apex court for civil and criminal matters.

apex_courts.name: Constitutional Court (Tribunal Constitucional)
apex_courts.role: Constitutional Court with original jurisdiction over constitutional review.

professional_regulators.name: Andorran Order of Psychologists (COPAND)
professional_regulators.role: Federal professional order for psychologists in Andorra.

anonymisation_convention: Andorran family-court decisions are anonymised per Superior Court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Pareatges 1278 + Co-principality establishment framework
key_developments.description: Pareatges (Acts of Co-sovereignty) 1278 between Bishop of Urgell and Count of Foix substantively distinctive globally only-co-principality-in-history framework. Substantive 700+ year Bishop-of-Urgell-+-Count-of-Foix/President-of-France co-sovereignty framework. Foundational pre-Constitution-1993 framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Borís Skósyrev affair + 11-day-kingdom framework
key_developments.description: Borís Skósyrev (Boris I) self-proclaimed king 8-20 July 1934 — substantively distinctive globally 11-day-kingdom-of-Andorra framework. Substantive pre-democratic-transition framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Modern Constitution + Council of Europe membership prep + UN-membership framework
key_developments.description: Modern Constitution adopted 14 March 1993 by referendum establishing democratic framework. UN admission 28 July 1993 — substantively significant micro-state-UN-membership framework. Andorra ratified the UNCRC 2 January 1996 — substantively significant child-rights framework integration.

key_developments.title: Council of Europe membership
key_developments.description: Andorra joined the Council of Europe 10 November 1994; ECHR became applicable. Substantively significant European human-rights-framework integration affecting subsequent family-law-jurisprudence.

key_developments.title: Civil Code (Codi de la Família) + Catalan-civil-law-substantive framework
key_developments.description: Federal Civil Code Family Code adopted 1995 drawing on Catalan civil-law substantive heritage. Arts. 27-58 govern potestat parental and child custody. Foundational substantive-civil-law framework persisting through subsequent reform-trajectory affecting family-law-implementation.

key_developments.title: EU Monetary Agreement + Euro-adoption framework
key_developments.description: Andorra Monetary Agreement with EU 30 June 2011 (after 2005 negotiations) substantively distinctive Western European micro-state Euro-adoption framework. Substantive cross-border-jurisdiction-practice framework affecting subsequent family-law-implementation including cross-border-asset-tracing.

key_developments.title: Hague Convention 1980 accession
key_developments.description: Andorra acceded to the Hague Convention 1980 effective 1 July 2011 — substantively significant Western European Hague-accession framework affecting cross-border-jurisdiction-practice with France and Spain.

key_developments.title: Qualified Law on Marriage 2017 + same-sex-marriage framework
key_developments.description: Qualified Law on Marriage 2017 (Llei Qualificada del Matrimoni) substantively reforming family-law framework — substantive marriage-equality framework affecting subsequent family-law-implementation framework. Substantively significant Western European Catholic-Co-principality post-marriage-equality framework.

key_developments.title: Law 14/2019 on Children's and Adolescents' Rights
key_developments.description: Federal Law on children's rights enacted aligned with UNCRC obligations. Substantively significant Western European micro-state child-protection-framework consolidation. Codifies superior-interest-of-the-child standard.

key_developments.title: Raonador del Ciutadà + EU association agreement framework + Catalan-language framework
key_developments.description: Raonador del Ciutadà (Ombudsperson) institutional position addressing PA-related issues within Civil Code + Law 14/2019 framework. EU association agreement framework negotiations 2023 substantively significant Western European micro-state institutional context. Catalan-language framework substantively significant for cross-border family-law adjudication with Spain (Catalonia) and France (Pyrénées-Orientales). Substantively significant Western European Pyrenean institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Tribunal Superior de Justícia + Tribunal Constitucional — superior-interest-of-the-child substantive register + Co-Princes-Macron-Vives framework
key_developments.description: Tribunal Superior de Justícia and Tribunal Constitucional continue to develop superior-interest-of-the-child jurisprudence under Civil Code arts. 27-58 + Law 14/2019 + Hague Convention 1980 framework in custody disputes within Co-Princes Emmanuel Macron (President of France since 2017) and Joan-Enric Vives Sicília (Bishop of Urgell since 2003) framework. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: Andorra operates a Catalan-civil-law family-law framework — places Andorra in the Catalan civil-law tradition cluster.
Co-principality constitutional structure (Bishop of Urgell + President of France as Co-Princes) is structurally distinctive globally — only co-principality in the corpus.
Council of Europe + ECHR + Hague Convention places Andorra in the European civil-law cluster.
Catalan official-language framework is structurally distinctive within Europe in the corpus.
editorial_notes: Andorra jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 4 to 10 key_developments with full Pareatges-to-Macron-Vives trajectory: 1278-Pareatges-+-Co-principality + 1934-Borís-Skósyrev-+-11-day-kingdom + 1993-Modern-Constitution-+-UN-+-UNCRC + 1994-Council-of-Europe-membership + 1995-Civil-Code-Codi-de-la-Família + 2005-EU-Monetary-Agreement-+-Euro-adoption + 2011-Hague-Convention-1980-accession + 2017-Qualified-Law-on-Marriage-+-same-sex-marriage + 2019-Law-14-2019-Children's-Rights + 2024-Tribunal-Superior-+-Tribunal-Constitucional-+-Co-Princes-Macron-Vives.
Civil-law Western European co-principality micro-state (Civil Code Catalan + Law 14/2019 Children's Rights + ECHR + Hague Convention 1980 accession 2011).

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive superior-interest-of-the-child analysis under Civil Code arts. 27-58 + Law 14/2019 + Hague Convention 1980 framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins Western-European + civil-law + Catalan-civil-law-substantive + ECHR + Hague-Convention + only-co-principality-in-history-globally-distinctive-Pareatges-1278-700-years-Bishop-of-Urgell-+-Count-of-Foix-then-President-of-France + Catalan-official-language-distinctive-Europe + Borís-Skósyrev-11-day-kingdom-1934-distinctive + EU-Monetary-Agreement-Euro-adoption-2011-+-micro-state + Qualified-Law-on-Marriage-2017-+-same-sex-marriage + Co-Princes-Macron-2017-+-Vives-Sicília-2003-Bishop-of-Urgell clusters within the corpus.
